profiles.name: Maya Okonkwo
profiles.headline: Design-systems lead who shipped AI-assisted Figma workflows at a fintech
contexts: Built an internal Figma plugin that auto-generates component variants
Scaled a 200-component system across 4 product teams
signature_takes: AI design tools should be opinionated, not blank-canvas
Design systems are the only place AI codegen is safe to ship today
profiles.wants_from_network: paid advisory with teams adopting AI into mature design systems
profiles.would_meet: an eval/research person who can measure AI-generated UI quality

profiles.name: Diego Fuentes
profiles.headline: AI-UX researcher measuring trust in generative interfaces
contexts: Ran 30+ studies on user trust in AI-suggested edits
Built a rubric for grading generative-UI output
signature_takes: Users forgive wrong AI suggestions but never slow ones
Confidence UI is a dark pattern unless the model is actually calibrated
profiles.wants_from_network: paid consulting on evaluating AI design features
profiles.would_meet: a design-systems lead shipping AI codegen who needs a quality rubric

profiles.name: Priya Sharma
profiles.headline: Prompt-to-UI engineer who built a production generative component system at a Series-C SaaS
contexts: Shipped a natural-language-to-component pipeline consuming GPT-4o and Tailwind tokens
Reduced design-to-code handoff from 3 days to 4 hours across 6 squads
signature_takes: Prompt-to-UI without a design token constraint layer produces unusable output 80% of the time
The real unlock is bidirectional sync: code updates should propagate back to Figma, not just forward
profiles.wants_from_network: co-founder or technical co-pilot for a prompt-to-UI devtool startup
profiles.would_meet: a design systems architect who has thought hard about token governance

profiles.name: Tomás Reyes
profiles.headline: Head of Design at an AI-native product studio, ex-Google Material Design
contexts: Rebuilt a 15-person design org around AI-first workflows in under 6 months
Led Material Design's experimental AI-assisted theming effort before leaving Google
signature_takes: Design orgs that don't kill at least one job title by end of 2025 are sleepwalking
AI shouldn't replace taste — it should expose who never had any
profiles.wants_from_network: advisory roles with design-tool startups or VC portfolio companies
profiles.would_meet: a researcher who can quantify the productivity delta of AI-first vs. traditional design orgs

profiles.name: Kenji Watanabe
profiles.headline: Accessibility engineer focused on AI-generated UI compliance gaps
contexts: Audited 12 AI-generated UI systems for WCAG 2.2 compliance; 11 had critical failures
Built an automated a11y gate that blocks AI codegen PRs failing contrast or ARIA rules
signature_takes: Every AI codegen tool I have tested ships inaccessible output by default — accessibility is not a prompt you can add later
Screen-reader compatibility is the canary in the coal mine for AI-generated UI quality
profiles.wants_from_network: consulting engagements with teams shipping AI-generated UI at scale
profiles.would_meet: a design systems lead who wants to bake a11y into their AI-codegen pipeline from day one

profiles.name: Layla Hassan
profiles.headline: Design ops director who rewired tooling procurement around AI readiness at a 500-person org
contexts: Ran an 18-month RFP that evaluated 9 AI design tools against a custom readiness framework
Reduced per-designer tool cost by 40% while increasing AI adoption from 10% to 75%
signature_takes: Most AI design tool evaluations are theater — teams buy on demos, ignore the governance gap, and regret it in 6 months
Design ops is now a competitive moat, not a back-office function
profiles.wants_from_network: fractional COO engagements with scaling design orgs or consulting on design-tool vendor selection
profiles.would_meet: an AI tool founder who wants honest enterprise feedback on their product's governance story

profiles.name: Amara Diallo
profiles.headline: Generative brand system designer who built an AI-maintained visual identity for a global CPG
contexts: Designed an AI-driven brand system that auto-adapts 1,200 assets across 40 markets
Built the brand-consistency scoring model that feeds back into the generation pipeline
signature_takes: Brand is the last defensible moat against commoditized AI output — but only if you treat it as a constraint system, not a mood board
Generative brand tools are still solving the wrong problem: variation without coherence
profiles.wants_from_network: paid brand-AI advisory with global enterprise clients or licensing the scoring model
profiles.would_meet: an AI researcher working on semantic consistency in image-generation models

profiles.name: Finn Andersen
profiles.headline: Motion designer who prototypes AI-driven micro-interaction systems
contexts: Built a GPT-4-driven Lottie generation pipeline that ships production animations from a single-sentence brief
Reduced animation production time by 70% at a B2C subscription app with 8M users
signature_takes: AI animation tools optimize for quantity and break the one rule that matters: timing should feel intentional, not averaged
Motion is the most underrated signal for AI-generated UI quality — wrong timing destroys trust faster than wrong color
profiles.wants_from_network: consulting on AI motion systems for product teams and brand studios
profiles.would_meet: a product designer who wants to integrate AI animation into a design system without losing craft control

profiles.name: Naledi Dlamini
profiles.headline: AI-UX research lead who built the user-insight pipeline for a generative design tool startup
contexts: Ran longitudinal studies tracking how designers' mental models shift after 90 days of AI tool use
Designed the feedback loop that feeds real user sessions back into model fine-tuning
signature_takes: Designers adopt AI tools for speed and quit for loss of agency — and no one is fixing the agency problem
Co-pilot framing is a cope: the moment the AI suggests something, the designer's decision is anchored, not free
profiles.wants_from_network: advisory or embedded research roles with AI design tool companies
profiles.would_meet: a design leader rethinking team structure after AI adoption flattened their junior pipeline

profiles.name: Rohan Mehta
profiles.headline: Design engineer who built the Figma-to-production bridge at a hypergrowth fintech
contexts: Shipped a Figma plugin that converts annotated frames to production React + Tailwind in one click
Maintained zero-drift between design tokens in Figma and tokens in the codebase for 2 years
signature_takes: The design-engineering gap is not a process problem — it is a data model problem; Figma's node tree and the DOM are fundamentally misaligned
AI codegen will stay a toy until it can reason about component state, not just component shape
profiles.wants_from_network: co-founder conversations for a design-engineering tooling company
profiles.would_meet: a product designer who has thought deeply about the data model that would make design-to-code actually reliable

profiles.name: Sofia Lindqvist
profiles.headline: Design systems architect who led AI governance for a 400-person enterprise design org
contexts: Authored the AI-generated UI quality policy adopted across a Fortune 500's design org
Built an LLM-as-judge pipeline that reviews every AI-generated component for brand, a11y, and token compliance before merge
signature_takes: You cannot govern AI-generated UI with a style guide — you need a machine-readable constraint language
Most enterprise AI design pilots fail because governance is an afterthought; the policy needs to exist before the pilot begins
profiles.wants_from_network: board advisory roles on AI governance in design or consulting with enterprises setting up similar frameworks
profiles.would_meet: an accessibility engineer who can stress-test the LLM-judge pipeline on real edge cases

profiles.name: Carlos Montoya
profiles.headline: AI product designer who shipped 3 zero-to-one AI-native products in 18 months
contexts: Designed the UX for an AI writing assistant that went from 0 to 200k DAU in 8 months
Developed a pattern library for AI interaction states covering loading, uncertainty, correction, and trust repair
signature_takes: Skeleton screens are a lie for AI products — they promise speed the model cannot deliver and destroy trust on day 2
AI products that hide uncertainty are designed for demos, not for users who come back on day 30
profiles.wants_from_network: freelance product design roles with early-stage AI startups
profiles.would_meet: a researcher who has studied user mental models of AI uncertainty and how to surface it without alarming

profiles.name: Yuki Tanaka
profiles.headline: Prompt engineer for design, specializing in text-to-image systems for commercial brand work
contexts: Developed a proprietary prompting framework used by 4 major ad agencies to produce on-brand campaign imagery at scale
Reduced creative iteration cycles from 2 weeks to 48 hours for a Fortune 100 consumer brand
signature_takes: Prompt libraries are a band-aid; what brands actually need is a semantic embedding of their visual identity that steers generation without manual prompting
Text-to-image for brand work will plateau until models can internalize brand constraints the way a senior creative director does
profiles.wants_from_network: partnerships with brand agencies or in-house creative teams scaling AI image production
profiles.would_meet: an AI researcher working on fine-tuning or LoRA approaches for brand-consistent image generation

profiles.name: Blessing Eze
profiles.headline: Design leadership coach helping senior designers navigate the AI disruption of their career paths
contexts: Coached 80+ senior designers at FAANG and growth-stage startups through AI-driven role redefinition
Developed a '5 AI design leadership archetypes' framework used by 3 design schools
signature_takes: The designers who will thrive are not the ones who are best at using AI tools — they are the ones who can articulate what AI cannot yet do
Most design teams are automating the wrong things first; they should automate quality checks before they automate creation
profiles.wants_from_network: keynote speaking, workshop facilitation, and executive coaching engagements
profiles.would_meet: a CHRO or VP Design at a company actively restructuring their design org around AI workflows

profiles.name: Miriam Novak
profiles.headline: UX engineer who built an AI design-review bot that catches design-system violations in PRs
contexts: Shipped a GitHub Actions bot that uses vision models to flag design-token and spacing violations in PR screenshots
Reduced design-system drift by 60% over 12 months at a mid-market SaaS with 30 engineers
signature_takes: LLM-as-judge for UI quality only works if the judge was trained on your specific system, not on general web patterns
Automated design review is not about replacing the designer — it is about catching the 80% of violations that happen because no one had time to look
profiles.wants_from_network: consulting with eng-heavy teams that have no dedicated design QA process
profiles.would_meet: a design systems lead who wants to integrate automated review into their CI pipeline

profiles.name: Adaeze Nwosu
profiles.headline: Researcher studying how AI changes power dynamics between designers and product managers
contexts: Embedded for 6 months in 4 product teams studying how AI tool adoption shifted decision authority between roles
Published 'The PM Veto Effect': how AI-generated mockups have reduced designer input in early roadmap decisions
signature_takes: AI design tools are inadvertently giving PMs a veto over design earlier in the process than is healthy for product quality
The real cost of AI-generated wireframes is not the quality of the output — it is the conversation that no longer happens before the output exists
profiles.wants_from_network: research partnerships with design tool companies wanting honest org-behavior data
profiles.would_meet: a design leader or exec who has lived through an AI-driven design org restructuring and is willing to talk about what went wrong

profiles.name: Jerome Castillo
profiles.headline: Frontend architect who standardized AI-assisted component generation across a multi-brand platform
contexts: Architected a shared component layer that lets AI generate brand-specific variants from a single semantic spec
Wrote the internal AI codegen standard now used by 5 independent engineering teams at a media conglomerate
signature_takes: Multi-brand AI codegen is unsolved because no one has cracked the semantic gap between 'brand A's primary button' and its token values at runtime
The engineers who will own AI codegen quality are the ones who understand design tokens as a type system, not just a naming convention
profiles.wants_from_network: advisory with platform teams building multi-brand or white-label products using AI codegen
profiles.would_meet: a design systems lead who has wrestled with token taxonomy at the multi-brand scale

profiles.name: Ingrid Svensson
profiles.headline: AI design ethicist advising on bias and representation in AI-generated imagery for consumer products
contexts: Led a 12-month audit of AI-generated people imagery across 6 consumer brands, surfacing demographic skew in all cases
Developed a representation rubric that has been adopted by 2 large agencies as a pre-launch checklist
signature_takes: AI image models do not have a diversity problem — they have an incentive problem; the companies training them have never been held accountable for the output
Representation audits run after launch are PR exercises; the only meaningful intervention is upstream, in the data curation pipeline
profiles.wants_from_network: consulting and advisory with brands, agencies, and AI tool companies on ethical image generation
profiles.would_meet: an AI researcher or ML engineer with access to training pipeline decisions who is willing to engage on the representation problem

profiles.name: Oumar Balde
profiles.headline: Design strategist who runs AI-in-design transformation programs for mid-market companies
contexts: Led 6 end-to-end AI design transformation engagements ranging from 3 to 18 months
Built a '90-day AI design readiness sprint' framework now licensed to 2 consultancies
signature_takes: AI transformation programs that start with tools fail; the ones that start with the workflow map succeed
The hardest part of AI design adoption is not the tooling — it is convincing senior designers that their years of craft instinct are still valuable in an AI-assisted world
profiles.wants_from_network: enterprise consulting pipeline and potential investors for a design-transformation SaaS
profiles.would_meet: a design ops leader inside a mid-market company who is 6 months into an AI rollout and hitting the hard part

profiles.name: Sione Taufa
profiles.headline: AI product designer specializing in voice and multimodal interface design
contexts: Designed the conversation flow and fallback UX for an AI voice agent handling 50k calls/day in financial services
Built the design system for a multimodal AI product combining voice, text, and structured UI components
signature_takes: Voice UI designers who come from visual design backgrounds over-engineer the silence — pauses are not a UX failure, they are load-bearing
Multimodal AI products fail at the modality switch: the moment a voice agent hands off to a screen is where 70% of trust is lost
profiles.wants_from_network: consulting with teams designing voice or multimodal AI products, especially in regulated industries
profiles.would_meet: a conversational AI researcher who has studied user mental models of voice agent identity and trust

requests.title: AI-assisted design workflow advisor
requests.problem: Our design system is mature and governed, but we keep stalling on how to bring AI into the workflow without breaking that governance.
requests.insights_wanted: How you rolled AI tooling into an established system, what guardrails you set, and what you'd do differently.
requests.need: Someone who has actually integrated AI into a mature design system without breaking governance
requests.why_fit_signal: shipped AI tooling inside an established design system; opinion that AI tools should be opinionated

requests.title: Pre-launch AI-UX evaluation
requests.problem: We ship our generative-UI product in six weeks and have no independent read on whether users actually trust the output.
requests.insights_wanted: A pre-launch evaluation across trust, usability, and speed, plus a rubric we can keep using.
requests.need: Independent researcher to run a pre-launch evaluation of their AI-generated component system across trust, usability, and speed dimensions
requests.why_fit_signal: experience running trust studies on AI-suggested UI; has a grading rubric for generative-UI output quality

requests.title: Audit of AI-generated UI quality
requests.problem: We're about to scale an AI-UI program org-wide and need an outside audit before we commit budget.
requests.insights_wanted: A quality, brand-compliance, and design-system-adherence audit, ideally with an automated or LLM-judge rubric.
requests.need: Expert to audit their AI-generated UI components for quality, brand compliance, accessibility, and design system adherence before they scale the program org-wide
requests.why_fit_signal: has shipped or evaluated AI-generated UI inside a design system at scale; ideally has an LLM-judge or automated rubric approach to quality measurement

requests.title: Scale AI image generation, keep brand identity
requests.industry: Global CPG brand · creative team of 20
requests.problem: AI image generation is fast but drifts off-brand. We need consistency without killing the speed.
requests.insights_wanted: How to build consistency scoring and constraint layers on top of generative image tools.
requests.need: Someone who has built a brand-consistent generative image system at scale and can advise on the consistency scoring and constraint layers
requests.why_fit_signal: built a generative brand system with scoring model; take that brand is a constraint system, not a mood board

requests.title: Accessibility review of AI codegen output
requests.problem: Our AI codegen pipeline ships UI fast, but accessibility is an afterthought and audits are failing.
requests.insights_wanted: A WCAG 2.2 review of generated components and help designing an a11y gate that runs in CI.
requests.need: Accessibility engineer to audit AI-generated UI components for WCAG 2.2 compliance and help design an a11y gate that runs in CI
requests.why_fit_signal: has audited AI-generated UI for accessibility compliance; has built automated a11y gates for codegen pipelines

requests.title: Stand up an AI design ops practice
requests.industry: Series-C health tech · design org of 30
requests.problem: Every team is adopting AI tools ad hoc and our ops layer can't keep up with the chaos.
requests.insights_wanted: How to define an AI design-ops practice: tooling, review rituals, and what to centralize vs leave to teams.
requests.need: Someone who has built design-ops structure around AI tooling across multiple product teams
requests.why_fit_signal: scaled design ops and AI tooling across several teams; opinionated on what to centralize

requests.title: Train a product team on AI-assisted design
requests.problem: Our designers are curious about AI tooling but nobody has shipped with it, so adoption keeps stalling.
requests.insights_wanted: A practical session on real workflows, what's worth adopting now, and how to get a team over the hump.
requests.need: A practitioner who can run a hands-on session on real AI-assisted design workflows
requests.why_fit_signal: has shipped real work with AI design tools and can teach the workflow, not just the theory

requests.title: Write a safe-AI-adoption playbook for our design org
requests.industry: Series-B health tech · design org of 25
requests.problem: Designers want to use AI coding tools but leadership is nervous about quality and things breaking in prod.
requests.insights_wanted: A practical playbook: what to allow, what guardrails to set, and how to roll it out without a mess.
requests.need: Someone who set up safe AI tooling for a design team and can write the rules of the road
requests.why_fit_signal: set guardrails for AI coding tools so a design team could adopt them safely

requests.title: Help us choose and roll out AI design tools
requests.problem: Too many AI design tools, no idea which to commit to or how to get the team actually using them.
requests.insights_wanted: Which tools are worth it for a small team, and how to drive real adoption rather than one-off experiments.
requests.need: A practitioner who has picked AI design tools and driven team-wide adoption
requests.why_fit_signal: evaluated AI design tools hands-on and got a whole team adopting them
